what_it_does: Implements a persistent, draggable floating "V" handle (the right-hand-man) that opens a fanned verb menu, hosts an Ask brain panel via a global forkVBrain attachment, provides a note composer and an open channel poster, tracks a contextual aim from projection events, and runs a one-time first-contact greeting plus a three-step guided tour.
observations: Heavy inline comments act as a design ledger, explaining seams, contracts, operator-law, and copy status.
The component owns the aim address and human label; forkVBrain owns the chat stream/reply rendering.
Verbs are split into live and soon sets, with the soon verbs hidden behind an honest 'What's coming' toggle.
Aim updates come from projection:select and projection:aim custom events; addressless activity events are marked unavailable rather than silently falling back to the surface.
The brain panel renders React-owned starter/tour/post UI above fork's appended .v-brain node.
Notes route through brain.direct to /api/territory/write; posts route through brain.postToChannel.
Drag position and first-contact greeting state are persisted in localStorage with try/catch degrade-clean handling.
Pointer capture/release is wrapped in try/catch as best-effort cleanup.
standards: The proactive first-contact greeting and 'Show me around' tour that auto-asks the first starter question.
Explicit 'soon' and unavailable states instead of green-painting unfinished or unaddressable features.
Fail-soft brain attachment: if forkVBrain is missing, the panel still renders and talk no-ops rather than crashing.
Operator-law: human labels and meanings are shown; raw addresses are never surfaced to the operator.
conventions: React functional component with hooks (useState, useRef, useEffect, useCallback).
Refs hold live values (aimRef, labelRef, surfaceAimRef) so callbacks read current state without rebinding.
Custom DOM events (projection:select, projection:aim, gallery:verb, gallery:write-error) are used as cross-module seams.
TypeScript const assertions for static verb/starter arrays.
Best-effort localStorage and fetch with degrade-clean error handling.
concerns: The fan layout uses hand-tuned magic numbers (slot heights, arc offsets) that may drift if styling changes.
onPost does not verify brainRef.current exists before calling postToChannel, so it can silently no-op if the brain failed to attach.
The setAim callback fires an async label fetch and updates refs/state; concurrent rapid aim changes could set a stale label if responses arrive out of order (no cancellation/abort of the fetch).
The note auto-close timer is stored in a ref and cleared manually in several places, which is easy to miss in future edits.
notes: This is the React host shell for the right-hand-man: it renders the handle, fan, note composer, and brain-panel container, and wires them to projection events and forkVBrain.
questions: What exactly does forkVBrain.attach expect in its configuration object beyond panelEl and the two getters?
How does the CSS for .vhandle-fan, .v-brain-panel, and .v-note position themselves relative to the handle?
What is the full shape of the projection:select and projection:aim event details across all emitters?
Is there a reason onPost does not guard against a missing brainRef like saveNote does?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is explicitly a draggable overlay handle with a verb fan, brain panel, note composer, channel poster, aim tracking, and first-contact onboarding.
apparent_themes: Honest feature status (live vs soon vs unavailable).
Fail-soft integration with an external brain fork.
Proactive, self-similar guidance at every level of aim.
Seam-based architecture meeting at data/events rather than cross-imports.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make a complex surface feel teachable and actionable from a single persistent corner companion, while being transparent about what it can and cannot do right now.
novelty: The combination of a draggable corner fan, an aim-aware guided three-question walk, an ungated channel poster, and explicit 'soon'/'unavailable' honesty in one self-contained React host.
contribution: A self-contained React host for the right-hand-man overlay that owns the contextual aim, renders the verb fan and operator-facing composers, and provides the attachment point and lifecycle for forkVBrain without cross-repo imports.
summary_for_embedding: RightHand.tsx is a React component that renders a draggable floating "V" handle exposing a fanned verb menu, an Ask brain panel with guided starter questions, a note composer, and an open channel poster, all anchored to a contextual aim address surfaced via projection events.
intention_for_embedding: To give operators a persistent, thumb-reachable guide that explains and acts on whatever they point at, while being honest about unavailable or upcoming capabilities.

symbols.does: Type alias for an optional draggable handle position.
symbols.description: Defines Pos as either an {x,y} screen-coordinate object or null, representing the saved or current resting place of the V handle.

symbols.does: Type alias for in-progress drag state.
symbols.description: Tracks the active pointer id, the offset from the handle's top-left, the pointer-down start coordinates, and whether movement has crossed the tap-vs-drag threshold.

symbols.does: Type alias for the forkVBrain attachment interface.
symbols.description: Describes the methods exposed by the attached brain, including ask, groundedAsk, postToChannel, direct, aimChanged, and destroy.

symbols.does: Renders the draggable right-hand-man handle and its fan, brain panel, note composer, and greeting.
symbols.description: Main React component that mounts the brain fork, listens to projection aim/select events, manages drag position, verb fan, Ask panel, note composer, channel post, and first-contact tour.

symbols.does: Resolves the global forkVBrain attachment factory.
symbols.description: Inside the mount effect, reads window.forkVBrain and, if present and a panel element exists, attaches the brain to the panel with live aim accessors.

symbols.does: Handler for projection:select events to update the V's aim.
symbols.description: Receives selection detail; if no detail resets to the surface aim, if an address is present aims there, otherwise treats addressless activity events as honestly unavailable.

symbols.does: Handler for projection:aim events to update the V's aim.
symbols.description: Receives aim detail with optional address, label, and meaning, then calls setAim to repoint the V and update human-readable labels.

symbols.does: Closes the fan, brain panel, and note composer.
symbols.description: Local helper inside the Escape/outside-click effect that clears the pending auto-close timer and resets the open states.

symbols.does: Keyboard listener that closes overlays on Escape.
symbols.description: Local helper that invokes closeAll when the Escape key is pressed while any overlay is open.
Checks whether the event target is outside the handle, panel, and note elements, then calls closeAll.

symbols.does: Marks the note composer status as error on gallery:write-error.
symbols.description: Local listener used while the note composer is open to surface route-back failures as an explicit error state rather than a silent no-op.
